news.headline: Messi becomes World Cup all-time leading scorer - how has he stayed at the top?
news.desc: As he becomes the top goalscorer in World Cup history, BBC Sport explores how Lionel Messi is still delivering on the biggest stage in his late 30s.

news.headline: The key differences between Tuchel and Southgate's England teams
news.desc: Analysing the similarities and differences between how Gareth Southgate and Thomas Tuchel led England.

news.headline: Fans delayed entry as bad weather affects France-Iraq tie
news.desc: Fans have been told not to travel to the Philadelphia Stadium for Monday's World Cup game between France and Iraq because of "inclement weather in the region".

news.headline: Ghana's Partey ready to play against England
news.desc: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey says he is ready to face England after being denied entry to Canada for their World Cup opener.

news.headline: Madueke's remarkable season - from petition to World Cup starter
news.desc: Noni Madueke has gone from fans protesting against him signing for Arsenal to England World Cup starter. BBC Sport look at his rollercoaster season.

news.headline: Doku returning to World Cup after birth of first child
news.desc: Jeremy Doku flew home to attend the birth of his first child - but why has that proved controversial in some quarters?

news.headline: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
news.desc: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

news.headline: Is VAR being used differently at the World Cup vs the Premier League?
news.desc: The stats show that there have been more VAR reviews at the World Cup than in the Premier League. So why does perception suggest the opposite?

news.headline: Iran leave note asking for peace after Belgium draw
news.desc: Iran leave a message saying "may peace, respect and friendship prevail among all nations" following their World Cup group game with Belgium.

news.headline: TV pundit Bogdanovic apologises for racist comments
news.desc: Former Atletico Madrid striker Rade Bogdanovic apologises for racist comments he made towards black players during a live World Cup broadcast on Serbian television.

news.headline: 'He's stored in a hard disk in my head' - watching the World Cup as a manager
news.desc: Former Tottenham and Brentford manager Thomas Frank on watching new players at the World Cup - who would he sign and who will be the star of the tournament?

news.headline: How Ferguson became Scotland's most influential World Cup player
news.desc: BBC Scotland examines the impact of Bologna's Lewis Ferguson in the Scotland midfield going into the crunch game with Brazil.
